entries: {@i Choose A or B:} (A) {@item Leather Armor|XPHB}, {@item Staff|XPHB|Arcane Focus (Staff)}, {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB}, {@item Scholar's Pack|XPHB}, and 5 GP; or (B) 50 GP

classFeature.name: Combat Supporter
entries: Once per turn when you cast a level 1+ Healer spell that targets an ally, that ally gains a +1 bonus to attack rolls and its saving throw DCs until the start of your next turn. If the spell targets multiple allies, you choose which one gains this benefit.
This bonus increases to +2 when you reach level 11, and to +3 when you reach level 17 as a Healer.

entries.name: Rules Clarification: Am I My Own Ally?
entries: For the purposes of this class' features, you do not count as your own ally, unless specified otherwise. The Healer is designed to be a support-oriented character, putting their allies ahead of themself.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: Your studies into the magic of vitality allow you to cast spells. The information below details how you use the rules of Spellcasting with Healer spells, which appear in the Healer spell list later in this class's description.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two cantrips of your choice from the Healer spell list. {@spell Buff|HealerClass24} and {@spell Guidance|XPHB} are recommended.
Whenever you gain a Healer level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the Healer spell list.
When you reach Healer levels 4 and 10, you learn another cantrip of your choice from the Healer spell list, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Healer Features table.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Healer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose four level 1 Healer spells. {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, {@spell Jump|XPHB}, {@spell Heroism|XPHB} and {@spell Shield|XPHB} are recommended.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Healer levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Healer Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Healer spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the Healer Features table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 3 Healer, your list of prepared spells can include six Healer spells of level 1 or 2 in any combination.

If another Healer feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Healer spells for you.

entries.name: Changing Your Prepared Spells
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one spell on your list with another Healer spell for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Healer spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an Arcane Focus as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Healer spells.

classFeature.name: Expert Physician
entries: Select one of the following skills: {@skill Medicine|XPHB}, {@skill Nature|XPHB}, {@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}, or {@skill Survival|XPHB}. You gain a bonus to ability checks you make with the chosen skill equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum bonus +1).

classFeature.name: Life Tether
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can create a magical tether of life energy which extends from your space and attaches to one ally you can see within 60 feet of yourself. The tether is severed if you end it (no action required), if you use this feature to attach a tether to a different creature, or if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. The tether is also severed if you are ever more than 60 feet away from the target or if you are separated by {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}. Your Life Tether has the following properties.

entries.name: Healer's Dice
entries: You have a pool of four d8s, called your Healer's Dice. You can only expend one of these dice at a time, and you regain all expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The number of Healer's Dice you have and the maximum number that you can expend at once both increase as you gain levels in this class, as shown in the Healer's Dice & Max Uses columns of the Healer Features table.

entries.name: Healing Tether
entries: When you attach the tether to a creature, or as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while it is attached, you can expend and roll a number of your Healer's Dice and restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to the tethered creature equal to the total rolled.
Alternatively, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to heal yourself in this way, even if the tether isn't attached.

entries.name: Life's Embrace
entries: You can target the tethered creature with your Healer spells as if it was within 5 feet of you, including spells with a range of 'Touch'.

entries.name: Healer's Protection
entries: When the tethered creature makes a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to grant it {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on that roll.

classFeature.name: Healer Subclass
entries: You gain a Healer subclass of your choice. Subclasses are detailed after this class' description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you special abilities at certain Healer levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass' features that are of your Healer level or lower.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Master Healer
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Dice Restoration
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you regain a number of expended Healer's Dice equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum one die).

entries.name: Extra Healing
entries: Whenever you restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to one or more creatures with a Healer spell, you can expend and roll a number of your Healer's Dice and add the result to the healing total.

entries.name: Vital Substition
entries: When you cast a {@filter Healer spell from the Abjuration or Necromancy school that has a Material component with a cost specified|spells|class=healer (hc'24)|components & miscellaneous=material is consumed;!reprinted|school=a;n}, you can expend Healer's Dice to ignore that material component. You must expend one die for every 50 GP of the component's cost. You are not limited in the number of dice you can expend in this way.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Healer Subclass.

classFeature.name: Restoring Tether
entries: When you use your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Healing Tether} on a creature, instead of restoring {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to it you can expend two Healer's Die to remove one of the following conditions from it: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB} or {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}.
Starting at level 13, you can expend three Healer's Dice to remove one of the following conditions instead: {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Empowering Tether
entries: The range of your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} increases to 90 feet.

In addition, while an ally is attached to your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2}, its attacks are empowered. Each time it hits with an attack, it can add {@dice 1d8} to the damage roll.

classFeature.name: Combat Supporter (+2)
entries: Your {@classFeature Combat Supporter|Healer|HealerClass24|1} bonus is +2.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Restoring Tether Improvement
entries: When you use your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Healing Tether} on a creature, instead of restoring {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to it you can expend three Healer's Dice to remove one of the following conditions instead: {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Healer Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Combat Supporter (+3)
entries: Your {@classFeature Combat Supporter|Healer|HealerClass24|1} bonus is +3.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Healer Subclass.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an {@filter Epic Boon feat|feats|category=EB} or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Spell Recall|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeature.name: Multitether
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can temporarily increase the number of {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Life Tethers} you can maintain to an amount equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 2 tethers). You can attach any number of these tethers to eligible creatures as part of the same {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Duration
entries: You can maintain multiple tethers in this way for 1 minute, or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. After this duration has elapsed, you can choose one tether to remain, if any still exist.

entries.name: Switching Targets
entries: If you use your {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to attach a tether to a new creature, you can do so with any number of tethers simultaneously.

entries.name: Shared Healing
entries: When you use {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Healing Tether} or {@classFeature Restoring Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|7} features, each tethered creature gains the benefit at no additional Healer's Dice cost.

entries.name: Uses
entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore its use by expending a level 6+ spell slot (no action required).

entries.name: Healer
entries: Healers study the magic of vitality, a power which they learn to manipulate with the ultimate altruistic intent - to preserve lives. Many Healers find occupations as physicians, combat medics, or researchers in medicine: healing the sick and the wounded using their powers for philanthropic or gainful employment. Some, however, find their talents best utilised by supporting others in a life of adventure.

For the life of an adventurer is inherently dangerous, constantly facing threats from nature, adversaries and the forces of evil, and greatly benefits from any available source of protection. While the role of the supporter is not the most glorious, Healers appreciate that it is often the most vital, for there is no glory in an avoidable death. Adventuring Healers excel at keeping their allies alive, treating their ailments, and augmenting their capabilities with their unique brand of magic.

entries.name: This is a Support Class
entries: Unlike other classes with elements that push characters toward a support role, the Healer is designed to solely occupy that role. It has no strictly offensive spells, and is not recommended for campaigns with only one player character.

subclassFeature.name: Vow of the Augmenter
entries: {@i Enhance the Capabilities of your Allies}

Healers who take the Vow of the Augmenter dedicate themselves to the study of life's potency. Dissatisfied with the limitations of their allies and themselves, they learn transformative magics which make their teammates stronger, faster and more effective at whatever role they claim. An adventurer never inhabits their full potential until they travel with one of these Healers.

subclassFeature.name: Augmenter Spells
entries: When you reach a Healer level specified in the Augmenter Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Augmenter Spells
rows: {@spell Bless|XPHB}, {@spell Buff|HealerClass24}, {@spell Enhance Ability|XPHB}, {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB}, {@spell Jump|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Enhancing Tether
entries: Whenever you use your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Healing Tether} feature, you can cast the {@spell Buff|HealerClass24} spell on that ally as part of the same {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Augmenter
entries: When you use your {@classFeature Combat Supporter|Healer|HealerClass24|1} feature, if the spell you cast on an ally requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, the ally gains the bonus to its attacks and DCs for the full duration of the spell, or until the start of your next turn if the spell ends before then.

subclassFeature.name: Dual Augmentation
entries: When you take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast one of the spells from your {@subclassFeature Augmenter Spells|Healer|HealerClass24|Augmenter|HealerClass24|3} feature, you can cast a second Augmenter spell that you have prepared as part of the same action, but using a separate spell slot. Each spell can only target a single, separate ally, but both targets gain the benefits of your {@classFeature Combat Supporter|Healer|HealerClass24|1} feature.
You can simultaneously concentrate on both spells; if you need to roll to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}, you only roll once, and lose both spells on a failure.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Greater Empowerment
entries: The damage die for your {@classFeature Empowering Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|10} increases to a {@dice d12}. Furthermore, creatures attached to your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on an attack roll with a {@dice d20} roll of one lower than normal.

subclassFeature.name: Master Augmenter
entries: When you target a creature with the {@spell Buff|HealerClass24} spell, it gains all three benefits at once, instead of you having to choose one.

subclassFeature.name: Vow of the Martyr
entries: {@i Exemplify Self Sacrifice}

Healers that take the Vow of the Martyr embody selflessness in its truest form. Through relentless study and practice, they wield powers to spare others from suffering, but at their own expense. The heroism of these Healers has inspired many great works of art, and continues to inspire acts of bravery on the battlefield.

subclassFeature.name: Martyr Spells
entries: When you reach a Healer level specified in the Martyr Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Martyr Spells
rows: {@spell Compelled Duel|XPHB}, {@spell Shield of Faith|XPHB}, {@spell Warding Bond|XPHB}, {@spell Zone of Truth|XPHB}
{@spell Circle of Power|XPHB}, {@spell Mass Cure Wounds|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Martyr's Endurance
entries: Your self-sacrificing nature improves your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} in the following ways.

entries.name: Endurance
entries: Whenever you use your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Healing Tether} on an ally, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the amount rolled on the Healer's Dice (round up).

entries.name: Anguish
entries: Whenever a creature that is attached to your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} takes damage, you can choose to reduce the damage it takes by half (round down), and take the other half of the damage yourself (no action required). The damage you take cannot be reduced in any way.

subclassFeature.name: Exemplar
entries: When you take damage in place of another creature through the use of a feature like Anguish or a spell like {@spell Warding Bond|XPHB}, your act of selflessness can inspire an ally of your choice within 60 feet of yourself that can see you (no action required). Until the end of its next turn, the ally has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and Wisdom saving throws. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until the start of your next turn.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Selfless Sacrifice
entries: You can cast {@spell Heroic Sacrifice|HealerClass24} by expending 4 Healer's Dice instead of a spell slot.

If you cast {@spell Heroic Sacrifice|HealerClass24} on a creature attached to your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the incurred saving throw. If you succeed on the save, the attached creature also gains {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} if it lacks it.

subclassFeature.name: True Martyrdom
entries: Your {@subclassFeature Martyr's Endurance|Healer|HealerClass24|Martyr|HealerClass24|3} feature improves, replacing the previous benefits.

entries.name: Endurance
entries: Whenever you use your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Healing Tether} on an ally, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the amount rolled on the Healer's Dice.

entries.name: Anguish
entries: Whenever a creature that is attached to your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} takes damage, you can choose to reduce the damage they take by any amount (you choose when you use this feature), and then you take damage equal to half the amount you reduced (round down, no action required). The damage you take cannot be reduced in any way.

subclassFeature.name: Vow of the Protector
entries: {@i Protect the Innocent, No Matter the Cost}

Healers who take the Vow of the Protector are honor-bound to protect their allies from danger in all forms that it takes. Their abjurative magic empowers their healing and conjures radiant barriers to shield their companions from every threat. Their companions rarely fall- and only after the world itself has tried.

subclassFeature.name: Protector Spells
entries: When you reach a Healer level specified in the Protector Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Protector Spells
rows: {@spell Aid|XPHB}, {@spell Prayer of Healing|XPHB}, {@spell Sanctuary|XPHB}, {@spell Shield|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Tether of Protection
entries: Whenever you use your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Healing Tether} on a creature, it gains a bonus to its saving throws until the start of your next turn. The bonus is equal to one plus the number of Healer's Dice you spent.

subclassFeature.name: Shield of the Protector
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Free Castings
entries: You can cast the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Protective Shield
entries: When a creature attached to your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} is hit by an attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cast {@spell Shield|XPHB}, granting the benefits of the spell to the target instead of yourself.

subclassFeature.name: Vigilant Protector
entries: When an ally within 60 feet of you is hit by an attack or makes a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to attach your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} to it. You can use your {@subclassFeature Shield of the Protector|Healer|HealerClass24|Protector|HealerClass24|3||Protective Shield} or {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Healer's Protection} as part of this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Warding Essence
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can choose two damage types, two conditions, or one of each from the lists below. You and creatures attached to your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the chosen damage types, and {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the chosen conditions until you use this feature again.

entries.name: Damage Types
entries: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Thunder.

entries.name: Conditions
entries: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, {@condition Prone|XPHB}, {@condition Restrained|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Bountiful Healing
entries: When you use the Extra Healing benefit of your {@classFeature Master Healer|Healer|HealerClass24|5} feature, you can replace one or more of the Healer's Dice with a {@dice d6}, instead of expending them.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Life Tether
entries: Your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} improves.

entries.name: Total Protection
entries: When you use your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Life Tether's} Healer's Protection {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can cause the ally to automatically succeed on the saving throw. If you are also making a saving throw against the same effect, you can also gain this benefit.
Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Expansive Ward
entries: You can now choose any damage type or condition for your {@subclassFeature Warding Essence|Healer|HealerClass24|Protector|HealerClass24|6} feature.

subclassFeature.name: Vow of the Siphon
entries: {@i Steal and Redistribute Foes' Vitality}

Healers who take the Vow of the Siphon see all living creatures as a bountiful source of energy, prime for reaping. Inspired by the medicinal and magical properties of leeches, they unearth dark powers which allow them to steal vitality from their enemies, leaving them weakened and vulnerable. While these dark practitioners are shunned from many Healer communities, their abilities prove indispensable to adventurers seeking to vanquish great foes.

subclassFeature.name: Siphon Spells
entries: When you reach a Healer level specified in the Siphon Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
In addition, Necromancy spells from the Wizard spell list are added to the Healer spell list for you.

entries.caption: Siphon Spells
rows: {@spell Bane|XPHB}, {@spell Blindness/Deafness|XPHB}, {@spell Hold Person|XPHB}, {@spell Inflict Wounds|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Harmful Tether
entries: You can attach your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} to foes, rather than allies, granting the following effects.

entries.name: Leeching Tether
entries: When you use {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2||Healing Tether}, you can deal an amount of Necrotic damage equal to the healing roll to the target instead of restoring {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to it.

entries.name: Sap Strength
entries: Whenever the target of your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} deals damage to another creature, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce that damage by an amount equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1 damage reduced).
Starting at Healer level 10, you can use this feature without using your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Life Leech
entries: When you use your {@subclassFeature Harmful Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|Siphon|HealerClass24|3||Leeching Tether} you can cause one creature of your choice within 15 feet of the target to regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage taken by the target (round up).

subclassFeature.name: Improved Sap Strength
entries: When you use your {@subclassFeature Harmful Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|Siphon|HealerClass24|3||Sap Strength}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to add half your Healer level (round up) to the amount of damage reduced.

subclassFeature.name: Sap Defenses
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can attempt to rob the defenses of one creature attached to your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2}. Choose one damage type. The target must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, it gains {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to the damage type you chose until the start of your next turn. This {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} bypasses {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}, but the target automatically succeeds on its save if it has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.
Once a creature fails its save against this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Leech Conduit
entries: You always have the {@spell Life Siphon|HealerClass24} spell prepared. While you are concentrating on {@spell Life Siphon|HealerClass24}, a creature attached to your {@classFeature Life Tether|Healer|HealerClass24|2} automatically fails its saves against the spell, and takes an additional {@dice 1d12} damage.

spell.name: Adrenaline Rush
entries: You briefly fill one creature you can see within range with surging energy. Until the start of your next turn, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 20 feet, and each time it hits with an attack roll and deals damage, it can add {@dice 1d10} to the damage roll.

spell.name: Buff
entries: You briefly enhance the capabilities of one creature other than yourself that you can see within range. Select one of the following enhancements, which lasts until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Amplified Attacks
entries: The next time the target hits with an attack roll and deals damage, it can add {@dice 1d8} to the damage roll.

entries.name: Fleeting Protection
entries: The target gains {@dice 1d8} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. These {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} vanish if any remain when the spell ends.

entries.name: Speedy Flight
entries: The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet and {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} are made against it with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage of Amplified Attacks and the {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} of Fleeting Protection increase by {@dice 1d8} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}) and 17 ({@dice 4d8}). The {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} boost of Speedy Flight also increases by 5 feet when you reach levels 5 (10 feet), 11 (15 feet) and 17 (20 feet).

spell.name: Elemental Body
entries: You infuse one creature you can see within range with elemental energy, transforming its physical form into a living embodiment of that element. Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Thunder, Radiant or Necrotic. The target gains the following benefits for the duration.

entries.name: Flight
entries: The target gains a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and can hover.

entries.name: Fluid Form
entries: The target can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so, and it can move through another creature's space and end its turn there. The first time the target enters a creature's space on a turn, that creature takes {@damage 3d8} damage of the type you chose.

entries.name: Immunities
entries: The target gains {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, {@condition Restrained|XPHB} and {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} conditions, and to the damage type you chose.

entries.name: Infused Attacks
entries: Whenever the target hits with an attack roll, it can deal an extra {@damage 2d8} damage of the type you chose to the attack's target.

spell.name: Exoskeleton
entries: You cause one creature you can see within range to grow a magically protective exoskeletal carapace. For the duration, the target has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to every damage type except for Psychic, and it cannot be moved or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition against its will.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature per spell slot level above 6.

spell.name: Greater Aid
entries: Choose up to four creatures within range. Each target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum and current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} increase by 50 for the duration. If a target is also benefiting from the {@spell Aid|XPHB} spell, that spell's effects are suppressed on the target until this spell ends on it.

spell.name: Greater Enhance Ability

components.m: A gilded tuft of fur or feather

entries: You touch a creature and choose Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma. For the duration, the target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} using the chosen ability.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 5. You can choose a different ability for each target.

spell.name: Helping Hand

time.condition: which you take when a creature within range makes a D20 Test

entries: You embolden the triggering creature, which can roll {@dice 1d8} and add the result to the {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}. You choose to cast this spell before the roll is made. If the roll is an attack roll, on a hit the attacker can also add the result of the {@dice d8} to the damage roll.

spell.name: Heroic Sacrifice

time.condition: which you take when you see another creature within range fail a saving throw against a spell or magical effect

entries: You interpose yourself against the triggering magic, causing it to affect you instead of the original target. The triggering creature is no longer affected by the spell or magical effect, and you become its target, even if you would normally be out of its range. You immediately make the required saving throw against the effect, sufferring its consequences on a failure. You do not suffer any effects on a successful save.

spell.name: Instant Refreshement

m.text: A fine beverage worth 200+ GP, which the spell consumes

entries: You touch a creature and flood it with healing energy, refreshing and rejuvinating its body and mind, causing it to instantly gain the benefits of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

spell.name: Jotunn Transformation

components.m: a piece of stone inscribed with a giant rune

entries: Select one creature you can see within range, which is infused with essence of a giant. The target gains the following benefits for the duration.

entries.name: Giant Size
entries: Its size increases to Huge, if it was not already larger. If there is no room for it to grow, the spell fails. The target's equipment grows with it. Attacks with {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} or a weapon enlarged in this manner deal an extra {@damage 1d12} damage.

entries.name: Giant's Strength
entries: Its Strength score increases to 11 plus twice the spell's level, if it was not already higher.

entries.name: Giant's Endurance
entries: It gains a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to 10 times the spell's level.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the Giant's Strength and Giant's Endurance benefits. If cast with a level 7+ slot, the target becomes Gargantuan instead of Huge and the extra damage increases to {@dice 2d12}.

spell.name: Life Siphon
entries: Choose up to four willing creatures that you can see within range. For the duration, at the end of each of a recipient's turns, it can select one creature that it can see within 30 feet of it. The selected creature must make a Consitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes {@damage 4d12} Radiant or Necrotic damage (your choice when you cast the spell) or half as much on a success. Success or failure, the recipient regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the amount of damage taken.
Once a creature takes this damage, it is immune to this spell's effects until the start of its next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d12} for each spell slot level above 7.

spell.name: Protection from Malady
entries: You ward one creature you can see within range against harmful effects. Select one of the following conditions: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} or {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}.

The target has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the chosen condition for the duration. If the creature already has the condition when you cast this spell, the condition does not end, but the target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any saving throw it makes to end that condition for the duration.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 4. You can choose a different condition for each target.

spell.name: Synergize
entries: Select two to three willing creatures that you can see within range. For the duration, each target is linked, making it more aware of and capable of helping its fellow linked creatures. Linked creatures gain the following benefits.

items: Whenever a linked creature fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, another linked creature within 30 feet of it can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to allow it to re-roll the test. It must use the new result.

When a linked creature rolls damage for an attack, it can add {@dice 1d6} to the damage roll for each other linked creature within 30 feet of it, as those allies contribute to the strike. It can do so only once per turn.

entries.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Total Refreshment

m.text: A fine beverage worth 999+ GP, which the spell consumes

entries: You channel immense healing energy into a creature you touch, instantly revitalising it. The target gains the benefits of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

Completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} in this way does not restore expended level 7+ spell slots, nor uses of features which allow a creature to cast a level 7+ spell without a spell slot (e.g. Mystic Arcanum from the Warlock class). Furthermore, once a creature is affected by this spell, it can't be affected by it again until it naturally finishes {@dice 1d4} Long Rests.

spell.name: Unlock Potential
entries: You touch a creature other than yourself and unlock the latent potential within it. Select either Unlocked Spellcasting or Unlocked Attacks. The target gains the benefits for the duration.

entries.name: Unlocked Spellcasting
entries: When the target casts a spell using a spell slot, it can treat the spell as if it was cast with a spell slot of one level higher. Spells cast with a level 5+ spell slot aren't affected.

entries.name: Unlocked Attacks
entries: When the target takes the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, it can make one additional attack as part of that action. In addition, it deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Force damage to the target whenever it hits with an attack roll.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can concentrate on the spell for longer: for 10 minutes (level 5-6 slot), for 1 hour (level 7 slot) or for 8 hours (level 8 slot). If you use a level 9 slot, the spell doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}, and its duration becomes 24 hours.
